calendar.matters:
Petition For Nomination Of Successor Trustee Of Irrevocable Trust
Off Calendar Dec-19-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Roger Marshall appeared by telephone for Catherine Murray.

Mr. Marshall stated that they have had no contact at all from Ms. Ritchie and that all of the paper work was lost in the Camp Fire. The Court noted that at the last hearing Mr. Murray was asked to provide a further declaration regarding due diligence. Mr. Murray stated he does not know anybody that has had any contact, that Ms. Murray is in her eighties and that Ms. Murray is unable to give him any more information. The Court stated that it believes sufficient efforts have been made to locate the trust, that it accepts Ms. Murray's testimony regarding the loss of the original trust document in the Camp Fire, that it takes into consideration equities of the situation and that basically if it were not to grant this, it would lead to a probate matter and Ms. Murray would end up being the sole Heir. The Court also noted that it does not know the terms of the trust document. The Court granted the Petition for Nomination of Successor Trustee of Irrevocable Trust (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Nomination Of Successor Trustee Of Irrevocable Trust
Off Calendar Oct-30-2019 Continued To Nov-27-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Nomination Of Successor Trustee Of Irrevocable Trust
Off Calendar Sep-18-2019 Continued To Oct-30-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Roger Marshall appeared by telephone for Catherine Murray (Petitioner).

The Court stated that it is a little of a big ask to appoint a Successor Trustee over a trust who's terms are entirely unknown, raised the issue of trying to track down another copy of the trust, spoke of Suzanne Cutts Ritchie's request to record the trust documents, and suggested that Ms. Ritchie may have a copy of the trust and that Counsel should try to contact her. Mr. Marshall stated that Ms. Murray told him that she had the original trust document and that it burned in the Camp Fire. The Court stated that it wants some assurance that all avenues were reasonably explored (attempt to contact Ms. Ritchie/attempt to locate any of her Dad's files/any other known Attorneys the Dad may have had, etc.), that it needs more information on Ms. Murray's due diligence and that it needs a declaration regarding her papers, any other locations where her Dad's paper may be, any other Attorneys that may have assisted her Dad, etc. The Petition for Nomination of Successor Trustee of Irrevocable Trust is continued to October 30, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Any supplemental documents are to be filed by October 23, 2019 and Mr. Marshall is to make sure he provides courtesy copies to the Court.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition For Nomination Of Successor Trustee Of Irrevocable Trust
